title: Container Creation, Process Lifecycle, CLI State, and Cleanup · Container Boundary Trace: Docker Foreground Detached Io
summary: A support utility must run repeatedly with explicit inputs, observable exit behavior, bounded names, and cleanup that never deletes unrelated state. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker foreground detached io increment in deterministic container lifecycle runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose foreground, detached, interactive, TTY, attach, and log behavior while preserving standard streams and exit evidence.
Apply names, environment, working directory, user, entrypoint, command, labels, hosts, and platform overrides with correct precedence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example operator inherits 5 similarly named containers: one created, one running, one exited with code 137, and one carrying the only writable-layer report. Inspect identity and state, preserve evidence, then perform label-scoped cleanup. Competency probe: Choose foreground, detached, interactive, TTY, attach, and log behavior while preserving standard streams and exit evidence. A defensible solution applies Docker Foreground Detached Io, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to deterministic container lifecycle runbook.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-containers-lifecycle-cli-2
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-containers-lifecycle-cli-2
    artifact: container-lifecycle.yaml
    decision: worked docker containers lifecycle cli 2
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: Docker Desktop VM with synchronized source files
    fault-injection: send termination during an in-flight durable operation
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      create: immutable-configured-container
      start: same-container-identity
      inspect: state-before-mutation
      cleanup: label-scoped-and-previewed
    verify: docker container inspect training-web --format json
    failure: Reject name-only cleanup, unbounded prune, or a claim that stop and remove preserve writable-layer state.
    repair: Use explicit IDs or labels, inspect before mutation, capture exit state, and remove only the scoped disposable resource.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Interactive and TTY flags are required for every process that reads standard input. This fails the Docker Foreground Detached Io contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
